message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #5 done, CI green, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #5 (HTMX inline editing) committed, pushed, CI GREEN (be0e44a, 65 tests).

NOTE: Carson pushed before consensus — this was a process deviation from the updated flow. We'll address this in the final process update to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md. For now, the code is pushed and CI is green.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review commit be0e44a and send consent or concerns. This is the last task — we need 9/9 consensus.

Changes:
- New template: todo_item_edit.html (inline edit form fragment)
- Modified: todo_item.html (HTMX attributes on edit link)
- Modified: routes/todos.rs (new get_todo_item handler, HTMX fragment responses for edit)
- Modified: startup.rs (new GET /todos/{id} route)
- Modified: todo-focus.js (focus management for edit mode)
- 4 new integration tests, 65 total
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me do a thorough review of this commit -- it's the most complex one.

content.content:
commit be0e44a984989032263c14243323a7dc729fdb2c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:45:01 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX inline editing for todo titles
    
    Clicking the edit pencil now swaps the todo item with an inline edit
    form via hx-get, without navigating to a separate page. Saving swaps
    back to the updated item view; Cancel restores the original item via
    GET /todos/{id}. Focus management: entering edit mode focuses the
    input, saving/canceling focuses the edit link. No-JS fallback still
    navigates to the full edit page.
    
    New endpoint: GET /todos/{id} returns a single todo item fragment.
    New template: todo_item_edit.html for the inline edit form.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 src/routes/mod.rs             |   3 +-
 src/routes/todos.rs           | 111 +++++++++++++++++---
 src/startup.rs                |   7 +-
 static/js/todo-focus.js       |  57 +++++++---
 templates/todo_item.html      |   1 +
 templates/todo_item_edit.html |  18 ++++
 tests/api/todos.rs            | 238 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 7 files changed, 401 insertions(+), 34 deletions(-)

content.text: Let me also check the cancel button's no-JS fallback behavior. The cancel button uses `hx-get` but is `type="button"` -- without JS/HTMX it would do nothing. Let me verify this is the intended progressive enhancement pattern.

content.text: Good. I now have a complete picture of the change. Let me send my thorough review.

input.content: **Review of be0e44a -- Kent Beck (TDD Coach)** **I consent**, with one non-blocking observation for the team to consider. **What changed (7 files, 401 lines added):** 1. `src/routes/todos.rs` -- `get_edit_todo` and `post_edit_todo` now check `HX-Request` header and return inline fragment vs full page. New `get_todo_item` handler returns a single item fragment (for cancel). New `TodoItemEditTemplate`. 2. `src/routes/mod.rs` -- re-exports `get_todo_item`. 3. `src/startup.rs` -- new `GET /todos/{id}` route. 4. `templates/todo_item_edit.html` -- new inline edit form fragment with `todo-item--editing` class. 5. `templates/todo_item.html` -- added `hx-get`, `hx-target`, `hx-swap="outerHTML"` to the edit link. 6. `static/js/todo-focus.js` -- focus management extended: entering edit mode focuses the input, exiting edit mode focuses the edit link. `pendingFocusTarget` upgraded from raw element to `{type, el/selector}` object for post-swap selector resolution. 7. `tests/api/todos.rs` -- 4 new tests (65 total): HTMX GET edit returns fragment, HTMX POST edit returns updated fragment with announce header, GET /todos/{id} returns item fragment, edit link has HTMX attributes. **TDD assessment -- strong test coverage:** - **4 new integration tests** cover the three HTMX request paths (GET edit, POST edit, GET item) and the template attributes. Each test verifies: correct status code, fragment (not full page), presence of expected content/classes, and absence of wrong state. - The `htmx_post_edit_returns_updated_item_fragment` test checks the `HX-Trigger` announce header -- verifying the aria-live announcement mechanism works for edits. Good. - Tests use the existing `extract_todo_id` helper and `register_and_login` pattern consistently. - All tests read as behavior specifications: "HTMX GET edit returns inline edit form fragment." Clear intent. **Design quality:** 1. **Progressive enhancement is correct.** Without JS: the edit link navigates to `/todos/{id}/edit` (full page form with cancel link back to `/todos`). With HTMX: the edit link swaps inline, cancel fetches the original item back. Both paths work. 2. **Cancel button approach is well-designed.** The cancel button (`type="button"` with `hx-get="/todos/{id}"`) fetches the original item fragment and swaps it back. This is better than trying to restore from client-side state -- the server is the source of truth. The new `get_todo_item` endpoint serves this purpose cleanly. 3. **Focus management evolution is smart.** Changing `pendingFocusTarget` from a raw element to `{type: "element" | "selector", ...}` is necessary because the edit form doesn't exist yet at `htmx:beforeSwap` time -- you need a selector to find the input *after* the swap settles. The response-sniffing approach (checking for `todo-item--editing` in `responseText`) is pragmatic. 4. **`htmx_response_with_announce`** is reused for the edit save path -- "Todo updated" announcement. Consistent with the existing pattern for add/toggle/delete. 5. **The inline edit template preserves the item's `id`** (`id="todo-{{ todo_id }}"`) so subsequent HTMX operations (save, cancel) can target the same element. This is critical for the swap chain to work. **Non-blocking observation:** The cancel `
input.summary: I consent to Task #5 commit be0e44a

content.text: Consent sent for Task #5. That completes all five tasks in this session. Standing by for whatever comes next -- whether that's a retrospective, process documentation update, or shutdown.
